tagline: Ebola has been building for months, Outside forces have made it far worse.

hero.title: Ebola Update—May 24
hero.author: Craig Spencer MD
hero.excerpt: Everything I Think You Need to Know
hero.brief:
-Outbreak ~2-3 months old, true count ~2,000. Second-largest ever
-No Bundibugyo vaccine. Ervebo and mAbs may help. Limited supply
-Treatment center burnings recur when responders ignore burial customs

selected.title: The Smartphone Minerals War That Destroyed Congo's Health System — Just in Time for Ebola
selected.author: Celine Gounder, MD
selected.sourceName: What I can and Can't Say on TV
selected.excerpt: Rwanda’s mineral extraction, America’s aid cuts, and the most money-driven conflict one epidemiologist has ever seen.
selected.brief:
The Story Behind Ebola
-USAID 2025 freeze plus armed conflict driven by demand for rare earth metals hits East Congo the same week
-Resulting destruction of health system caused 300,000 excess deaths over the past year
-Then Ebola hit

selected.title: The Burden of Caution
selected.author: Bruce Lanphear, MD
selected.excerpt: When scientific humility becomes public confusion
selected.brief:
Epidemiology Buries the Lede
-Epidemiology relies on cautious language re causality
-Tobacco playbook weaponizes scientific caution against science. Asbestos, lead, climate followed the same arc.
-Sometimes the honest word is "causes."

selected.title: American contracts Ebola in DRC, headed to Germany with other exposed Americans. Expert analysis.
selected.author: Jeremy Faust, MD
selected.excerpt: Dr. Boghuma Titanji and I analyze the Trump administration’s new travel restrictions, the decision to send exposed Americans to Germany, and what the outbreak reveals about US readiness.
selected.brief:
US Isolation
-Trump blocks entry of infected American doctor and family
- US bans non-US passport holders from DRC, Uganda, Sudan against WHO guidance
- US withdrawal means WHO can mobilize only $500K from its emergency fund

recent.title: The Fluoride Ruling Was Vacated—But the Science Wasn’t
recent.author: Bruce Lanphear, MD
recent.excerpt: The recent Ninth Circuit ruling on fluoride changed the legal trajectory of the case, but it did not resolve the scientific debate.

recent.title: Why Citizenship-Based Travel Bans Fail the Precautionary Principle
recent.author: Celine Gounder, MD
recent.sourceName: What I can and Can't Say on TV
recent.excerpt: From HIV to COVID to Ebola: the precautionary principle has been applied more aggressively to foreigners than to citizens for decades.

recent.title: Misinformation Masterclass
recent.author: Bob Morris, MD, PhD
recent.excerpt: A Wall Street Journal editor packs a pandemic’s worth of confusion into just 102 words
